articles.headline: Supreme Court Delivers Landmark Verdict on Environmental Clearance for Mega Projects
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court ruled on the interpretation of 'public interest' in granting environmental clearances, emphasizing ecological sustainability over purely economic considerations.
• The judgment mandates stricter scrutiny for projects in ecologically sensitive zones and strengthens the role of local communities in the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process.
• It calls for a 'Green Bench' within the National Green Tribunal (NGT) with enhanced powers to review compliance and impose penalties.
• The ruling is expected to have significant implications for infrastructure development, mining, and industrial projects, particularly in biodiversity hotspots.
• It reiterates the 'polluter pays' principle and the 'precautionary principle' as fundamental to environmental jurisprudence.

articles.trivia: India was one of the first countries to make environmental protection a constitutional directive through the 42nd Amendment in 1976.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Signals Cautious Stance Amid Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The MPC cited persistent global inflationary pressures and geopolitical uncertainties as key reasons for maintaining a 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• RBI Governor highlighted robust domestic demand and strong capital expenditure as factors supporting economic growth, projecting GDP growth at 7.0% for FY25.
• Concerns were raised about potential food price volatility due to erratic weather patterns and global commodity price fluctuations.
• The central bank emphasized its commitment to bringing inflation within the target band of 2-6% while supporting growth.

articles.headline: India, Japan Elevate Strategic Partnership with Focus on Indo-Pacific Security and Green Technologies
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Summit concluded with both nations agreeing to deepen cooperation across defense, critical and emerging technologies, and clean energy sectors.
• A joint statement emphasized shared commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, reiterating the importance of maritime security and adherence to international law.
• New initiatives were announced for collaboration in green hydrogen, renewable energy storage, and semiconductor supply chain resilience.
• Discussions also covered enhancing connectivity projects in third countries and strengthening QUAD cooperation.
• Both leaders underscored the need for greater people-to-people exchanges and cultural understanding.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test for Gaganyaan Mission's Crew Escape System
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a major milestone by successfully conducting the integrated test of the Crew Escape System (CES) for the Gaganyaan mission.
• The test, named 'Pad Abort Test 2 (PAT-2)', simulated an emergency scenario requiring the crew module to rapidly separate from the launch vehicle.
• This system is crucial for ensuring the safety of astronauts in case of an anomaly during the initial phases of the launch.
• The CES involves a set of solid-propellant rockets that can propel the crew module to a safe distance and altitude, followed by parachute deployment for a soft landing.
• This successful test brings India closer to its ambitious human spaceflight mission, Gaganyaan, planned for 2026.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes Amendments to Juvenile Justice Act, Emphasizing Rehabilitation and Child Rights
articles.explainer:
• Both Houses of Parliament passed the Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Amendment Bill, 2025, which aims to further strengthen the protection of children.
• Key amendments include streamlining adoption procedures, enhancing the safety of children in institutional care, and introducing stricter penalties for crimes against children.
• The bill mandates regular inspections of child care institutions and establishes a robust grievance redressal mechanism for children.
• It also introduces provisions for 'pre-adoption foster care' to ensure better matching of children with prospective adoptive parents.
• The focus remains on the best interests of the child, with an emphasis on rehabilitation, reintegration, and ensuring a safe environment.

articles.headline: National Green Tribunal Imposes Stricter Norms for Waste Management in Urban Areas
articles.explainer:
• The National Green Tribunal (NGT) issued fresh directives, mandating urban local bodies to adopt advanced waste-to-energy technologies and significantly improve segregation at source.
• The ruling emphasizes the need for decentralized waste processing units and imposes hefty fines on municipalities failing to comply with Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016.
• It calls for a time-bound action plan for remediation of existing legacy waste dumpsites and prevention of new illegal dumping.
• The NGT also directed state pollution control boards to conduct regular audits and submit compliance reports.
• This move aims to tackle the growing menace of urban waste and its adverse environmental and health impacts.

articles.headline: India Strengthens Coastal Surveillance with New Radar Network Installation
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) inaugurated a new phase of the Coastal Surveillance Network (CSN) with advanced radar stations and electro-optic sensors along key stretches of the coastline.
• This upgrade aims to enhance real-time maritime domain awareness, particularly in countering illicit activities like smuggling, illegal fishing, and terror infiltration.
• The integrated network will provide comprehensive coverage of India's vast coastline and island territories, improving response times for maritime emergencies.
• It incorporates Artificial Intelligence (AI) for anomaly detection and data fusion from various sensors, including drones and satellite imagery.
• The initiative is part of India's broader strategy to bolster maritime security and protect its economic and strategic interests in the Indian Ocean Region.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Saksham Yuva' Initiative for Skill Development and Employment Generation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched 'Saksham Yuva', a comprehensive initiative aimed at upskilling and reskilling India's youth for future-ready jobs.
• The program integrates various existing skill development schemes under a single framework, focusing on demand-driven training in emerging technologies like AI, IoT, and data analytics.
• It includes provisions for industry partnerships, apprenticeships, and financial assistance for entrepreneurship, targeting both urban and rural youth.
• A dedicated digital platform has been created to connect skilled youth with employment opportunities and provide career counseling.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the skill gap in India's workforce and boost the employability of its demographic dividend.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme Expanded to Boost Domestic Manufacturing of Advanced Drones and Components
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme has been extended to cover the manufacturing of advanced drones and their critical components, including sensors, propulsion systems, and control units.
• This expansion aims to make India a global hub for drone manufacturing, reducing reliance on imports and fostering indigenous innovation.
• Incentives will be provided to eligible manufacturers based on incremental sales over a base year, encouraging higher domestic value addition.
• The move is expected to create significant employment opportunities and bolster India's capabilities in sectors like defense, agriculture, logistics, and surveillance.
• It aligns with the government's 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives, promoting self-reliance in cutting-edge technologies.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Global South's Interests at G20 Sherpa Meeting on Climate Finance
articles.explainer:
• India's Sherpa to the G20 reiterated the country's commitment to advocating for the concerns of the Global South, particularly on issues of climate finance and technology transfer.
• During the G20 Sherpa meeting, India emphasized the need for developed nations to fulfill their climate finance commitments and increase support for adaptation and mitigation efforts in developing countries.
• India called for a 'just and equitable' transition that takes into account the varying developmental stages and capacities of different nations.
• Discussions also focused on reforming multilateral development banks to better address climate challenges and facilitate easier access to green funding.
• The meeting laid the groundwork for the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit, aiming to forge consensus on key global economic and environmental issues.

articles.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Chip Manufacturing Announced, Paving Way for Semiconductor Independence
articles.explainer:
• An Indian consortium, supported by the government's India Semiconductor Mission, announced a significant breakthrough in the indigenous manufacturing of 28nm semiconductor chips.
• This achievement marks a crucial step towards India's goal of achieving self-reliance in the critical semiconductor industry, vital for electronics, defense, and automotive sectors.
• The fabrication unit is expected to commence mass production by late 2025, creating thousands of high-tech jobs and attracting further investments.
• The government highlighted the importance of a robust semiconductor ecosystem, including design, testing, and packaging capabilities, to support this initiative.
• This development is seen as a game-changer for India's digital economy and national security, reducing dependence on volatile global supply chains.

articles.headline: Election Commission Reviews Voter ID Linkage with Aadhaar, Ensures Data Privacy Compliance
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) announced a comprehensive review of the ongoing program to link voter ID cards with Aadhaar, focusing on strengthening data privacy protocols.
• The ECI reiterated that Aadhaar linkage is voluntary and aims to prevent duplicate entries and ensure electoral roll purity, not to disenfranchise voters.
• A committee of experts has been formed to recommend enhanced security measures for data handling and storage, addressing concerns raised by privacy advocates.
• The review comes amidst ongoing debates about the balance between electoral integrity and individual privacy rights.
• The ECI emphasized its commitment to transparency and securing voter data, while also ensuring a clean and fair electoral process.

articles.headline: New Study Highlights Impact of Changing Monsoon Patterns on Indian Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• A recent study published by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM) reveals significant shifts in monsoon onset and withdrawal dates, impacting agricultural cycles across India.
• The research indicates an increase in extreme rainfall events and prolonged dry spells in certain regions, leading to crop losses and water stress.
• It projects a further intensification of these anomalies, posing serious challenges to food security and farmer livelihoods in the coming decades.
• The study recommends urgent adaptation strategies, including climate-resilient crop varieties, improved irrigation techniques, and early warning systems for farmers.
• This highlights the critical need for integrating climate change considerations into India's agricultural policies and rural development programs.

articles.headline: Union Budget Implementation Review: Focus on Capital Expenditure and Infrastructure Push
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance released its mid-term review of the Union Budget 2024-25, highlighting significant progress in capital expenditure (Capex) allocation and utilization.
• The review indicated that over 60% of the budgeted Capex has been utilized in the first three quarters, primarily in roads, railways, and urban infrastructure projects.
• This robust Capex push is seen as a key driver for economic growth, generating employment, and crowding in private investment.
• Challenges identified include delays in land acquisition for some projects and the need for better inter-ministerial coordination.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to fiscal prudence while continuing to prioritize infrastructure development for long-term economic prosperity.

articles.headline: National Health Mission Report Shows Progress in Maternal and Child Health Indicators
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare released its annual report on the National Health Mission (NHM), indicating significant improvements in key maternal and child health indicators.
• The report highlights a sustained decline in Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR) and Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) across several states, attributed to enhanced institutional deliveries and immunization coverage.
• Focus areas for further improvement include addressing malnutrition among children and women, and expanding access to primary healthcare in remote areas.
• The NHM continues to be the flagship program for strengthening public health infrastructure and service delivery in India.
• The findings underscore the importance of sustained investment in public health and community-level interventions.

articles.headline: Ministry of Home Affairs Unveils New Strategy to Combat Cybercrime Syndicates
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) announced a comprehensive national strategy to counter the growing threat of organized cybercrime syndicates targeting individuals, businesses, and critical infrastructure.
• The strategy focuses on enhancing inter-agency coordination, strengthening forensic capabilities, and fostering international cooperation to track down cross-border cybercriminals.
• It includes provisions for rapid response teams, a dedicated cybercrime intelligence unit, and public awareness campaigns on digital safety.
• The initiative also emphasizes upgrading the skills of law enforcement personnel in cyber forensics and digital investigation techniques.
• This proactive approach aims to create a secure cyberspace and protect citizens from various forms of online fraud and data breaches.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Discovers Ancient Buddhist Site in Eastern India
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a significant ancient Buddhist monastic complex during excavations in the coastal regions of Odisha.
• The site includes remnants of stupas, viharas, and intricate sculptures depicting scenes from the life of Buddha, dating back to the 7th-9th centuries CE.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism in Eastern India and its interactions with regional cultures during the early medieval period.
• Artifacts recovered include pottery, coins, and inscriptions in ancient scripts, providing valuable insights into the socio-economic and religious practices of the era.
• The ASI plans further excavations and conservation efforts to preserve this important cultural heritage site and make it accessible for research and tourism.

articles.headline: India to Host Global Health Summit, Focus on Pandemic Preparedness and Equitable Vaccine Access
articles.explainer:
• New Delhi is set to host a major Global Health Summit in late 2025, bringing together leaders, scientists, and health experts from around the world.
• The summit's agenda will primarily focus on strengthening global pandemic preparedness mechanisms, ensuring equitable access to vaccines and therapeutics, and building resilient health systems.
• India, having played a crucial role in vaccine manufacturing during the recent pandemic, aims to champion a 'One Health' approach, integrating human, animal, and environmental health.
• Discussions will also cover financing for global health initiatives and leveraging digital health solutions for universal health coverage.
• This initiative underscores India's growing leadership in global health diplomacy and its commitment to addressing shared health challenges.

articles.headline: Government Releases AI Ethics Guidelines for Healthcare Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a comprehensive set of ethical guidelines for the development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the healthcare sector.
• The guidelines emphasize principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, data privacy, and human oversight in AI-driven medical applications.
• They aim to foster innovation while ensuring patient safety, preventing algorithmic bias, and protecting sensitive health data.
• Recommendations include mandatory impact assessments for high-risk AI systems and the establishment of an independent regulatory body for AI ethics.
• This move is crucial for building trust in AI technologies and promoting their responsible integration into India's evolving healthcare landscape.

articles.headline: State Election Commission Announces Dates for Local Body Polls in Maharashtra
articles.explainer:
• The Maharashtra State Election Commission (SEC) announced the schedule for the upcoming local body elections, including Municipal Corporations, Zila Parishads, and Gram Panchayats.
• The polls are expected to be held in three phases over April and May 2025, with strict adherence to Model Code of Conduct guidelines.
• The SEC highlighted its preparedness to conduct fair and transparent elections, leveraging EVMs and VVPATs, and ensuring voter awareness campaigns.
• This election is crucial for local governance, as it will determine the leadership and policies at the grassroots level.
• The announcement triggers political activity and campaigning, focusing on local issues like infrastructure, sanitation, and public services.

articles.headline: UN Report Warns of Increasing Marine Heatwaves in Indian Ocean, Threatening Biodiversity
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) highlights a significant increase in the frequency and intensity of marine heatwaves in the Indian Ocean.
• These heatwaves are causing widespread coral bleaching, disruption of marine ecosystems, and threatening biodiversity, including fish stocks vital for coastal communities.
• The report links this phenomenon to global climate change and calls for urgent action to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and enhance marine conservation efforts.
• It warns of severe long-term impacts on the 'Blue Economy' sectors, such as fisheries and tourism, affecting millions of livelihoods.
• Recommendations include establishing more Marine Protected Areas (MPAs) and developing climate-resilient coastal management plans.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent Supreme Court judgment on environmental clearances, consider the following statements:
1. The judgment mandates stricter scrutiny for projects in ecologically sensitive zones.
2. It strengthens the role of state governments exclusively in the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process.
3. It reiterates the 'polluter pays' and 'precautionary' principles as fundamental to environmental jurisprudence.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the judgment mandates stricter scrutiny for projects in ecologically sensitive zones. Statement 2 is incorrect; the judgment strengthens the role of local communities, not exclusively state governments, in the EIA process. Statement 3 is correct as it reiterates the 'polluter pays' principle and the 'precautionary principle' as fundamental to environmental jurisprudence.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for drone manufacturing is/are correct?
1. The scheme aims to make India a global hub for drone manufacturing.
2. Incentives are provided based on incremental sales over a base year.
3. It focuses only on military-grade drones and their components.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; the PLI scheme aims to make India a global hub for drone manufacturing. Statement 2 is correct; incentives are provided based on incremental sales over a base year. Statement 3 is incorrect; the scheme targets advanced drones and their critical components, applicable to various sectors including defense, agriculture, and logistics, not exclusively military.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's human spaceflight mission, Gaganyaan:
1. The Crew Escape System (CES) is designed to ensure astronaut safety during the initial phases of launch.
2. The recent 'Pad Abort Test' simulated an emergency separation of the crew module from the launch vehicle.
3. The Gaganyaan mission aims to send astronauts into a Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit (GTO).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; the CES is crucial for astronaut safety during launch anomalies. Statement 2 is correct; the 'Pad Abort Test' simulates an emergency scenario for crew module separation. Statement 3 is incorrect; the Gaganyaan mission aims to send astronauts into a Low Earth Orbit (LEO), not a Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit (GTO).

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following best describes the 'One Health' approach advocated by India at the Global Health Summit?
options: (a) Focusing solely on human health and disease prevention.
(b) Integrating human, animal, and environmental health for a holistic approach.
(c) Prioritizing the health of developed nations over developing ones.
(d) Emphasizing only traditional medicine systems for global health issues.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Integrating human, animal, and environmental health for a holistic approach.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'One Health' approach recognizes that the health of humans, animals, and the environment are interconnected, and advocates for collaborative, multi-sectoral efforts to achieve optimal health outcomes.

prelimsMocks.question: With respect to the Election Commission of India's review of Voter ID-Aadhaar linkage, which of the following statements is correct?
options: (a) Aadhaar linkage is now mandatory for all voters to prevent duplication.
(b) The ECI has formed an expert committee to strengthen data privacy protocols.
(c) The primary goal is to disenfranchise voters with multiple entries.
(d) The review has concluded that there are no privacy concerns with the linkage.

Statement (a) is incorrect; the linkage remains voluntary. Statement (c) is incorrect; the goal is electoral roll purity, not disenfranchisement. Statement (d) is incorrect; the review specifically addresses concerns raised by privacy advocates.

GS1.headline: New Study Highlights Impact of Changing Monsoon Patterns on Indian Agriculture
GS1.explainer:
• A recent study published by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM) reveals significant shifts in monsoon onset and withdrawal dates, impacting agricultural cycles across India.
• The research indicates an increase in extreme rainfall events and prolonged dry spells in certain regions, leading to crop losses and water stress.
• It projects a further intensification of these anomalies, posing serious challenges to food security and farmer livelihoods in the coming decades.
• The study recommends urgent adaptation strategies, including climate-resilient crop varieties, improved irrigation techniques, and early warning systems for farmers.
• This highlights the critical need for integrating climate change considerations into India's agricultural policies and rural development programs.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Discovers Ancient Buddhist Site in Eastern India
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a significant ancient Buddhist monastic complex during excavations in the coastal regions of Odisha.
• The site includes remnants of stupas, viharas, and intricate sculptures depicting scenes from the life of Buddha, dating back to the 7th-9th centuries CE.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism in Eastern India and its interactions with regional cultures during the early medieval period.
• Artifacts recovered include pottery, coins, and inscriptions in ancient scripts, providing valuable insights into the socio-economic and religious practices of the era.
• The ASI plans further excavations and conservation efforts to preserve this important cultural heritage site and make it accessible for research and tourism.

• New initiatives were announced for collaboration in green hydrogen, renewable energy storage, and semiconductor supply chain resilience.

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes Amendments to Juvenile Justice Act, Emphasizing Rehabilitation and Child Rights
GS2.explainer:
• Both Houses of Parliament passed the Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Amendment Bill, 2025, which aims to further strengthen the protection of children.
• Key amendments include streamlining adoption procedures, enhancing the safety of children in institutional care, and introducing stricter penalties for crimes against children.
• The bill mandates regular inspections of child care institutions and establishes a robust grievance redressal mechanism for children.
• It also introduces provisions for 'pre-adoption foster care' to ensure better matching of children with prospective adoptive parents.
• The focus remains on the best interests of the child, with an emphasis on rehabilitation, reintegration, and ensuring a safe environment.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Saksham Yuva' Initiative for Skill Development and Employment Generation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched 'Saksham Yuva', a comprehensive initiative aimed at upskilling and reskilling India's youth for future-ready jobs.
• The program integrates various existing skill development schemes under a single framework, focusing on demand-driven training in emerging technologies like AI, IoT, and data analytics.
• It includes provisions for industry partnerships, apprenticeships, and financial assistance for entrepreneurship, targeting both urban and rural youth.
• A dedicated digital platform has been created to connect skilled youth with employment opportunities and provide career counseling.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the skill gap in India's workforce and boost the employability of its demographic dividend.

GS2.explainer:

• During the G20 Sherpa meeting, India emphasized the need for developed nations to fulfill their climate finance commitments and increase support for adaptation and mitigation efforts in developing countries.
• India called for a 'just and equitable' transition that takes into account the varying developmental stages and capacities of different nations.
• Discussions also focused on reforming multilateral development banks to better address climate challenges and facilitate easier access to green funding.
• The meeting laid the groundwork for the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit, aiming to forge consensus on key global economic and environmental issues.

GS2.headline: National Health Mission Report Shows Progress in Maternal and Child Health Indicators
GS2.explainer:

• Focus areas for further improvement include addressing malnutrition among children and women, and expanding access to primary healthcare in remote areas.
• The NHM continues to be the flagship program for strengthening public health infrastructure and service delivery in India.
• The findings underscore the importance of sustained investment in public health and community-level interventions.

GS3.headline: Supreme Court Delivers Landmark Verdict on Environmental Clearance for Mega Projects
GS3.explainer:
• The Supreme Court ruled on the interpretation of 'public interest' in granting environmental clearances, emphasizing ecological sustainability over purely economic considerations.
• The judgment mandates stricter scrutiny for projects in ecologically sensitive zones and strengthens the role of local communities in the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process.
• It calls for a 'Green Bench' within the National Green Tribunal (NGT) with enhanced powers to review compliance and impose penalties.
• The ruling is expected to have significant implications for infrastructure development, mining, and industrial projects, particularly in biodiversity hotspots.
• It reiterates the 'polluter pays' principle and the 'precautionary principle' as fundamental to environmental jurisprudence.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Signals Cautious Stance Amid Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The MPC cited persistent global inflationary pressures and geopolitical uncertainties as key reasons for maintaining a 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• RBI Governor highlighted robust domestic demand and strong capital expenditure as factors supporting economic growth, projecting GDP growth at 7.0% for FY25.
• Concerns were raised about potential food price volatility due to erratic weather patterns and global commodity price fluctuations.
• The central bank emphasized its commitment to bringing inflation within the target band of 2-6% while supporting growth.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test for Gaganyaan Mission's Crew Escape System
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a major milestone by successfully conducting the integrated test of the Crew Escape System (CES) for the Gaganyaan mission.
• The test, named 'Pad Abort Test 2 (PAT-2)', simulated an emergency scenario requiring the crew module to rapidly separate from the launch vehicle.
• This system is crucial for ensuring the safety of astronauts in case of an anomaly during the initial phases of the launch.
• The CES involves a set of solid-propellant rockets that can propel the crew module to a safe distance and altitude, followed by parachute deployment for a soft landing.
• This successful test brings India closer to its ambitious human spaceflight mission, Gaganyaan, planned for 2026.

GS3.headline: National Green Tribunal Imposes Stricter Norms for Waste Management in Urban Areas
GS3.explainer:
• The National Green Tribunal (NGT) issued fresh directives, mandating urban local bodies to adopt advanced waste-to-energy technologies and significantly improve segregation at source.
• The ruling emphasizes the need for decentralized waste processing units and imposes hefty fines on municipalities failing to comply with Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016.
• It calls for a time-bound action plan for remediation of existing legacy waste dumpsites and prevention of new illegal dumping.
• The NGT also directed state pollution control boards to conduct regular audits and submit compliance reports.
• This move aims to tackle the growing menace of urban waste and its adverse environmental and health impacts.

GS3.headline: India Strengthens Coastal Surveillance with New Radar Network Installation
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) inaugurated a new phase of the Coastal Surveillance Network (CSN) with advanced radar stations and electro-optic sensors along key stretches of the coastline.
• This upgrade aims to enhance real-time maritime domain awareness, particularly in countering illicit activities like smuggling, illegal fishing, and terror infiltration.
• The integrated network will provide comprehensive coverage of India's vast coastline and island territories, improving response times for maritime emergencies.
• It incorporates Artificial Intelligence (AI) for anomaly detection and data fusion from various sensors, including drones and satellite imagery.
• The initiative is part of India's broader strategy to bolster maritime security and protect its economic and strategic interests in the Indian Ocean Region.

GS3.headline: PLI Scheme Expanded to Boost Domestic Manufacturing of Advanced Drones and Components
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme has been extended to cover the manufacturing of advanced drones and their critical components, including sensors, propulsion systems, and control units.
• This expansion aims to make India a global hub for drone manufacturing, reducing reliance on imports and fostering indigenous innovation.
• Incentives will be provided to eligible manufacturers based on incremental sales over a base year, encouraging higher domestic value addition.
• The move is expected to create significant employment opportunities and bolster India's capabilities in sectors like defense, agriculture, logistics, and surveillance.
• It aligns with the government's 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives, promoting self-reliance in cutting-edge technologies.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Chip Manufacturing Announced, Paving Way for Semiconductor Independence
GS3.explainer:
• An Indian consortium, supported by the government's India Semiconductor Mission, announced a significant breakthrough in the indigenous manufacturing of 28nm semiconductor chips.
• This achievement marks a crucial step towards India's goal of achieving self-reliance in the critical semiconductor industry, vital for electronics, defense, and automotive sectors.
• The fabrication unit is expected to commence mass production by late 2025, creating thousands of high-tech jobs and attracting further investments.
• The government highlighted the importance of a robust semiconductor ecosystem, including design, testing, and packaging capabilities, to support this initiative.
• This development is seen as a game-changer for India's digital economy and national security, reducing dependence on volatile global supply chains.

GS3.headline: Union Budget Implementation Review: Focus on Capital Expenditure and Infrastructure Push
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance released its mid-term review of the Union Budget 2024-25, highlighting significant progress in capital expenditure (Capex) allocation and utilization.
• The review indicated that over 60% of the budgeted Capex has been utilized in the first three quarters, primarily in roads, railways, and urban infrastructure projects.
• This robust Capex push is seen as a key driver for economic growth, generating employment, and crowding in private investment.
• Challenges identified include delays in land acquisition for some projects and the need for better inter-ministerial coordination.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to fiscal prudence while continuing to prioritize infrastructure development for long-term economic prosperity.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Home Affairs Unveils New Strategy to Combat Cybercrime Syndicates
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) announced a comprehensive national strategy to counter the growing threat of organized cybercrime syndicates targeting individuals, businesses, and critical infrastructure.
• The strategy focuses on enhancing inter-agency coordination, strengthening forensic capabilities, and fostering international cooperation to track down cross-border cybercriminals.
• It includes provisions for rapid response teams, a dedicated cybercrime intelligence unit, and public awareness campaigns on digital safety.
• The initiative also emphasizes upgrading the skills of law enforcement personnel in cyber forensics and digital investigation techniques.
• This proactive approach aims to create a secure cyberspace and protect citizens from various forms of online fraud and data breaches.

GS3.headline: Government Releases AI Ethics Guidelines for Healthcare Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a comprehensive set of ethical guidelines for the development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the healthcare sector.
• The guidelines emphasize principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, data privacy, and human oversight in AI-driven medical applications.
• They aim to foster innovation while ensuring patient safety, preventing algorithmic bias, and protecting sensitive health data.
• Recommendations include mandatory impact assessments for high-risk AI systems and the establishment of an independent regulatory body for AI ethics.
• This move is crucial for building trust in AI technologies and promoting their responsible integration into India's evolving healthcare landscape.

GS3.headline: UN Report Warns of Increasing Marine Heatwaves in Indian Ocean, Threatening Biodiversity
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) highlights a significant increase in the frequency and intensity of marine heatwaves in the Indian Ocean.
• These heatwaves are causing widespread coral bleaching, disruption of marine ecosystems, and threatening biodiversity, including fish stocks vital for coastal communities.
• The report links this phenomenon to global climate change and calls for urgent action to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and enhance marine conservation efforts.
• It warns of severe long-term impacts on the 'Blue Economy' sectors, such as fisheries and tourism, affecting millions of livelihoods.
• Recommendations include establishing more Marine Protected Areas (MPAs) and developing climate-resilient coastal management plans.
